summary: Bug carded: auto-retry never engaged on RunPod "does not have the resources" refusal (scarce 5090 / Any-region); narrow out-of-stock regex missed the wording in 2 places.

Fix shipped: shared _isStockRefusal() in shell.js + MpiSettings.js, broadened to RunPod wordings; regex self-check + 16/16 hardening tests + eslint pass. Maturity validating — live RunPod verify owed.
Corrected: card had real in-flight work + live-verify owed, belongs in doing/in-progress not done. (Was wrongly jumped todo->done.)
3 fixes on disk: (1) _isStockRefusal regex, (2) wait-loop 15s backoff (de-spin), (3) panel status creating->connecting (4 spots). Logic-verified; live verify owed after app restart.

Consolidated: MPI-138 (dup-wait guard) merged in as Defect 4. Fixes 1-2 live-verified (retry engages, ~15s spacing). Defect 5 (button flicker every 15s) OPEN -- root-caused to Settings retry calling LOUD _initRemoteBoot per tick; needs deliberate redesign (silent per-retry create). Single owner for the whole auto-retry-connect issue.

Defect 5 (flicker) FIXED on disk (Option A — silent retry). shell.js: (a) _startGpuWait no longer nulls remoteWaitGpu before onFree; (b) _initRemoteBoot announces 'connecting'/'Creating' only via _announceConnecting once a real podId lands (warm announces immediately); (c) refused create backs off INLINE (15s) keeping remoteWaitGpu pinned — replaced the nested _startGpuWait that bailed under the active Settings wait and killed retry after one refusal. Panel's only flicker driver is remoteWaitGpu toggling; it now stays set through all refusals -> panel holds 'waiting…' steady, single waiting->connecting transition when Pod is real. Self-check waitloop.mjs 5/5 PASS; hardening 16/16; eslint clean. LIVE verify still owed (need scarce 5090).

REVERT-TO-SPEC refactor (user-directed). Live test of the Option-A patch FAILED: panel died to 'stopped'/Connect after retries; logs showed TWO drivers (settings + shell auto-connect-on-start) both calling createPod ~15s offset, racing remoteWaitGpu = self-cancel. Root cause traced to docs/gotchas.md L9-15 spec: the wait must be a SINGLE-owner loop; Defect-2's create-every-tick had spawned a second driver. User confirmed the requirement: low/medium-availability cards REFUSE create even when the flag reads available, so the create attempt IS the only honest probe — must keep retrying silently with Cancel+waiting message, NOT a read-only availability poll (that would wait forever). FIX: consolidated to a single owner. _initRemoteBoot is now THE connect+retry loop and self-guards single-flight (_gpuWaitActive/_gpuWaitFor): duplicate same-GPU call no-ops, different GPU aborts+takes over. Its create-loop refusal path now paints the steady waiting state via _enterWaitState (idempotent, phase:null) + 15s backoff + _retryContinues() (cancel/intent check) and loops — no nested wait. DELETED _startGpuWait + _runSettingsWait (the second driver). Bridge remote:wait-start now just calls _initRemoteBoot. _announceConnecting fires only on a real podId/starting. Self-check waitloop.mjs (single-owner + no-flicker + cancel) 6/6 PASS; hardening 16/16; eslint clean. App was not running; rebuilt code on disk. LIVE verify still owed on fresh launch.

Live test of the single-owner refactor: panel CORRECT (steady 'waiting…'+Cancel, no flicker, no self-cancel) — single-owner fix confirmed working. BUT a second RunPod refusal wording leaked: 'create pod: Something went wrong. Please try again later or contact support.' (HTTP 500, no Pod, scarce 5090) was NOT in _isStockRefusal -> threw -> 'Could not create a Pod' error dialog instead of retrying. RunPod returns >1 string for the same no-capacity condition. FIX: added 'something went wrong|try again later' to _isStockRefusal (both shell.js + MpiSettings.js copies). stockcheck.mjs self-check 13/13 (8 retryable incl. both 5090 wordings + 5 real-errors still surface: API key/offline/volume/internal-error/empty); eslint clean. LIVE re-verify owed: both refusal wordings now keep panel on 'waiting…', dialog only on genuine config/auth failure.

Live: retry loop CLEAN (log shows steady ~15s refused-retry, single owner, no double-loop). But the HERO FOOTER intermittently flashed 'CONNECTING · OFFLINE 0%' then dropped to LOCAL while the panel correctly stayed 'waiting…'. Root cause: backend /remote/pod/create sets _connecting=true for the ~1.5s the create REST is in flight (remoteProxy.js L710/740); the connection-feed poll (5s) landing in that window emitted phase:'connecting' (shell.js feed L955). Every 15s retry = a fresh ~1.5s connecting blip the hero could sample. Panel was already immune (gated on _isWaiting). FIX: in the feed's !connected branch, when state.remoteWaitGpu is set (auto-retry wait = no Pod exists) force phase:null + local·offline and clear any stale 'connecting', BEFORE the connecting->phase emit. Hero now stays local through the wait; real 'connecting' only shows once a Pod exists (_announceConnecting). eslint clean; waitloop 6/6 + stockcheck 13/13 still pass. LIVE re-verify owed: hero stays LOCAL + panel stays waiting, no CONNECTING flash, across several minutes.

Maturity -> validating (stays in 'doing' column; board has no validating column). All fixes committed-ready on disk + live-confirmed on a scarce RTX 5090: steady waiting/Cancel, no flicker/self-cancel/dialog/hero-flash; in-stock cards connect immediately. One open to-do (checklist.md): verify an ephemeral RTX 3090 (lower-variability) connects cleanly — proxy for the only unwitnessed path (a scarce card FREEING mid-wait -> clean flip to ready). 5090 stayed refused all session (suspected host maintenance while still listing capacity). Win edge shares the proven in-stock connect flow; high confidence, just unobserved.
